original_poem:
सारे जहाँ से अच्छा, हिन्दोसिता हमारा,  
हम बुलबुलें हैं इसकी, ये गुलसितां हमारा।  

ग़ुर्बत में हों अगर हम, रहता है दिल वतन में,  
समझो वहीं हमें भी दिल हो जहाँ हमारा।  

मज़हब नहीं सिखाता आपस में बैर रखना,  
हिंदी हैं हम, वतन है हिन्दोस्तां हमारा।
translated_poem:
Better than the entire world, is our Hindustan,  
We are its nightingales, and it’s our garden land.  

Even if we are in exile, our hearts reside at home,  
Wherever our hearts may be, there lies our homeland.  

Religion does not teach us to harbor enmity,  
We are Indians, and India is our motherland.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

translation_note.value: Rendered as Hindustan, preserving the geographic-cultural identity of the subcontinent.
Translated as home or homeland; watan carries an emotionally charged patriotic-devotional weight in Urdu-Hindi tradition not fully conveyed.
Translated as religion, which is adequate but loses the specific Urdu connotation of sectarian religious identity.
Translated as Indians; here Hindi refers to inhabitants of Hind, not the language, a distinction the translation preserves contextually.
